final_response: Drop it in today's daybook Log section as a capture — not tmp/ (gitignored, you'd lose it) and not a project yet (single TLDR, topic hasn't materialized). If it comes up next week as expected, promote to projects/prompt-cache-attacks/ and pull the PDF into that project's resources/. Captured at daybook/2026-05-17.md with a routing note for next week.

actions.summary: Created today's daybook with the TLDR captured in the Log section, plus a routing note: promote to projects/prompt-cache-attacks/ if the topic actualizes next week, or move to top-level resources/ if it remains reference-only.

notes: Triage choice: daybook Log over tmp/ because tmp/ is gitignored and the user explicitly said they want to keep it.
Did not create a project folder — a single TLDR for a paper not yet retrieved doesn't meet the 'more than one session's worth of material' threshold in projects/AGENTS.md.

Did not add a followups.md entry — the convention there is waiting-on items with an expected return from someone else, not self-reminders. The 'next week' anchor is captured in the daybook routing note instead.
Preserved the TLDR verbatim as a blockquote and flagged provenance ('research-assistant generated, PDF not yet retrieved') so a future reader doesn't mistake the summary for the paper itself.
